mechanism_narrative: A sustained level of the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound suppresses the equity discount rate and supports positive risk asset returns over the subsequent month as liquidity constraints ease.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 6% signals accelerating labor market deterioration that crushes consumer spending expectations and forces rapid earnings multiple compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling industrial production and collapsing housing starts reveals a synchronized contraction in goods production and residential investment that overwhelms any monetary offset, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the interaction term and 20-day forward returns is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During acute demand collapse episodes, a month-over-month decline in industrial production transmits directly through corporate revenue surprises, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets price in sequential profit shortfalls.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of weeks following negative INDPRO surprises during demand collapse regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in real retail sales below prior cycle trends signals collapsing consumer purchasing power that drags corporate revenues lower, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns fail to materialize in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS declines exceed 2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth triggers a fundamental repricing of cyclical earnings potential, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as investors extrapolate contraction into forward guidance.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns follow negative GDPC1 prints in more than 50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, liquidity stress remains elevated and policy space is exhausted, amplifying downside equity risk over the next week as negative shocks cannot be offset by conventional easing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate confirms a discontinuous labor market rupture rather than a gradual adjustment, forcing rapid de-risking and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to outperform either single predictor in explaining 20-day return variance (incremental R-squared < 0.01).
